notes: Verified via WebSearch 2026-07-11 (Wikipedia x2, Emarat Al Youm, Al Wafd): April 17, 1946 — last French soldier left Syrian territory; confirmed official holiday status.

Verified a rare, well-documented detail: France proposed leaving April 18 (Good Friday) 1946, but Syrian officials chose one day earlier specifically to avoid the evacuation celebration coinciding with a Christian religious holiday.
Explicitly distinguished from Syria's Independence Day declaration (March 8, 1920, 26 years earlier) — a common source of confusion in Arabic content.

Confirmed this holiday predates and is unaffected by the recent Presidential Decree No. 188 (which added revolution-day-syria/liberation-day-syria and removed 4 other holidays) — no mention of changes to April 17 in that decree.
Single-country event (_countryCode: sy), aliasSlugs: [] to avoid base_country_leakage; neutral historical register maintained consistent with the site's other Syria events.
prisoners-day-palestine not yet built — used palestine-independence-day and jordan-independence-day instead in relatedSlugs to avoid related_slug_not_found.
